name: Smokey Robinson
description: American R&B singer, songwriter and producer (born 1940)
abstract: William "Smokey" Robinson Jr. (born February 19, 1940) is an American R&B and soul singer, songwriter, and record producer.

concerts.label: Rainforest Foundation Benefit Concert
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Smokey Robinson
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

works_created.t: The Tracks of My Tears/ The Tears of a Clown
My Girl/ I Will Bless the Lord At All Times

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I did a lot of the rock and roll dates for Motown. I was on first call over there, I did a lot of things with Diana Ross and the Supremes, the Temptations, Smokey Robinson, Jackson Five, Michael Jackson…
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: I was doing a lot of stuff for Motown then, Smokey Robinson and Berry Gordy and all this kind of stuff. So I wanted to do — I was doing Motown sessions as a producer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Barbutti
about_quotes.evidence: they booked me at the Steel Pier in Atlantic City opening for Smokey Robinson and the Miracles
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Barbutti interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, December 8, 2022
